@@ -4466,6 +4466,93 @@ SELECT * FROM t_add_generated ORDER BY a
+# CREATE TABLE form with SEQUENCE NAME runs through the legacy CREATE TABLE
+# code path under both schema changers.
+CREATE TABLE t_seqname_create (id INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME my_create_seq))
+# Sanity: dropping the identity-owned sequence directly without CASCADE is
+# rejected; DROP TABLE is the supported way to clean up both the table and its
+statement error pq: cannot drop sequence my_create_seq because other objects depend on it
+# DROP SEQUENCE CASCADE on an identity-owned sequence: CRDB cascades through
+# and drops the sequence (note: this differs from PG, which rejects CASCADE
+# on a sequence that backs an identity column with the same "column requires
+# it" error). This is pre-existing behavior shared with auto-named identity
+# sequences and is unrelated to the SEQUENCE NAME clause.
+CREATE TABLE t_seqname_cascade (id INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME my_cascade_seq))
+# SEQUENCE NAME is not allowed in CREATE SEQUENCE / ALTER SEQUENCE.
+statement error pq: SEQUENCE NAME is only valid for identity columns
+statement error pq: SEQUENCE NAME is only valid for identity columns
+# Under the legacy schema changer, ALTER TABLE ... ADD GENERATED ... AS IDENTITY
+# (SEQUENCE NAME ...) errors out and the user must use the declarative schema
+CREATE TABLE t_seqname_legacy (a INT NOT NULL, FAMILY (a))
+statement error pq: ALTER TABLE \.\.\. ADD GENERATED \.\.\. AS IDENTITY \(SEQUENCE NAME \.\.\.\) requires the declarative schema changer
+ALTER TABLE t_seqname_legacy ALTER COLUMN a ADD GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (SEQUENCE NAME nope)
a int GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY (START WITH 2 INCREMENT 3),
+                                 // SEQUENCE NAME is only valid as a GENERATED AS IDENTITY option;

// SequenceOptions represents a list of sequence options.
+// ExtractIdentitySeqName returns the explicit sequence name supplied via the
+// PG18 SEQUENCE NAME clause (if any) and a copy of the option list with that
+// entry removed. The SEQUENCE NAME entry is metadata about how to name the
+// sequence and never makes it into the persisted sequence options.
+	// NameVal carries the explicit sequence name supplied by the
+	// PG18 SEQUENCE NAME clause when this option appears on a
+	// GENERATED AS IDENTITY column. It is meaningless in other
// Names of options on CREATE SEQUENCE.
+	// SeqOptName is the PG18 "SEQUENCE NAME <name>" option for
+	// identity columns. It is only valid inside the parenthesized
+	// option list of GENERATED { ALWAYS | BY DEFAULT } AS IDENTITY.
